body:
Fixes #1680

## Summary
- Persist active Telegram work-session metadata to `~/.hive-mind/telegram-active-sessions.json`, configurable with `HIVE_MIND_SESSION_STORE_PATH`.
- Reload persisted session metadata and run an immediate monitor pass when Telegram session monitoring starts, so completed `--isolation screen` runs can update the original message after a bot restart.
- Add a regression test plus the requested case-study artifacts under `docs/case-studies/issue-1680/`.

## Reproduction
The issue report showed a `/codex` screen-isolated solve stuck at `Solve command executing...` while `$ --status 6a0ec9c3-04b5-4c22-acc2-8f21e934036e` returned terminal status `executed` with exit code 0. The monitor could poll authoritative `$ --status`, but the Telegram `chatId`/`messageId` mapping lived only in memory, so a bot restart during a detached screen run lost the destination needed to edit the original message.

## Verification
- `npm test` (`/tmp/hive-mind-issue-1680-npm-test-final.log`)
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run format:check`
- `GITHUB_BASE_REF=main node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs`
- `bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh`
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh`

## Case Study
See `docs/case-studies/issue-1680/README.md` for timeline, root cause, local artifacts, and online references.
title: Persist Telegram session monitor state for screen isolation
